_comment: Parakeet per-tensor tolerances for compare_tensors.py.
ENTRIES BELOW THE BLANK LINE: finalized for parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v2/v3
(Stage 4 calibrated against observed C++ drift). Reference: NeMo
(nvidia/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v2/v3) running f32 inference; C++: ggml CPU
f32 compute, weights stored as f32 in GGUF.
Dominant divergence source: STFT precision. C++ uses fp64 STFT,
NeMo uses fp32. This produces ~5 max_abs in the mel, which the
subsampling conv amplifies. With dump tensors preserved as graph
outputs, observed CPU/F32 drift is v2: pre_encode ~127 max_abs /
v3: pre_encode ~699 / ~1.35, block0 ~27 / ~0.04, block12
~24 / ~0.08. The 24-layer conformer attenuates the drift:
block 23/final are ~0.04 (v2) / ~0.03 (v3).
LSTM decoder states are encoder-independent (zero input, zero
state at step 0) and match to fp32 round-off (~1e-7).
dec.joint.0 amplifies the encoder drift through the joint
projection (~70 max_abs on values ~1e3).
ENTRIES MARKED _provisional: true: added by Stage 2 oracle for the
8 new parakeet variants (ctc-0.6b/1.1b, rnnt-0.6b/1.1b, tdt-1.1b,
tdt_ctc-110m/1.1b, unified-en-0.6b). Magnitude-aware provisional
values: max_abs = max(1e-4 * p99_abs, 1e-6); mean_abs =
max(1e-5 * rms, 1e-6); p99_abs and rms taken as the max across all
variants and cases where the tensor appears. Stage 4 finalizes
these against observed C++ drift per port. Do NOT ship a model
while _provisional entries remain.
NOTE on per-variant scoping: this file is keyed flat by tensor
name, not by variant. The 14 finalized entries above will be the
starting tolerances for the 8 new variants too. Most are loose
enough; LSTM/embed entries are tight (1e-6) because LSTM start
state is computed from zeros and matches to fp32 round-off
regardless of variant.
